root_text.an6.15:1.1: Tatra kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an6.15:1.2: “tathā tathāvuso, bhikkhu vihāraṁ kappeti yathā yathāssa vihāraṁ kappayato kālakiriyā anutappā hoti.
root_text.an6.15:1.3: Kathañcāvuso, bhikkhu tathā tathā vihāraṁ kappeti yathā yathāssa vihāraṁ kappayato kālakiriyā anutappā hoti?
root_text.an6.15:2.1: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu kammārāmo hoti kammarato kammārāmataṁ anuyutto, bhassārāmo hoti …
root_text.an6.15:2.6: Evaṁ kho, āvuso, bhikkhu tathā tathā vihāraṁ kappeti yathā yathāssa vihāraṁ kappayato kālakiriyā anutappā hoti.
root_text.an6.15:2.8: ‘bhikkhu sakkāyābhirato nappajahāsi sakkāyaṁ sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāya’.
root_text.an6.15:3.1: Tathā tathāvuso, bhikkhu vihāraṁ kappeti yathā yathāssa vihāraṁ kappayato kālakiriyā ananutappā hoti.
root_text.an6.15:3.2: Kathañcāvuso, bhikkhu tathā tathā vihāraṁ kappeti yathā yathāssa vihāraṁ kappayato kālakiriyā ananutappā hoti?
root_text.an6.15:4.1: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu na kammārāmo hoti na kammarato na kammārāmataṁ anuyutto, na bhassārāmo hoti …
root_text.an6.15:4.5: na papañcārāmo hoti na papañcarato na papañcārāmataṁ anuyutto.
root_text.an6.15:4.6: Evaṁ kho, āvuso, bhikkhu tathā tathā vihāraṁ kappeti yathā yathāssa vihāraṁ kappayato kālakiriyā ananutappā hoti.
root_text.an6.15:4.8: ‘bhikkhu nibbānābhirato pajahāsi sakkāyaṁ sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāyā’ti.

translation_text.an6.15:1.2: “As a mendicant makes their bed, so they must lie in it, and die tormented by regrets.
translation_text.an6.15:1.3: And how do they die tormented by regrets?
translation_text.an6.15:2.1: Take a mendicant who relishes work, talk, sleep, company, closeness, and proliferation. They love these things and like to relish them.
translation_text.an6.15:2.6: A mendicant who makes their bed like this must lie in it, and die tormented by regrets.
translation_text.an6.15:2.8: a mendicant who enjoys substantial reality, who hasn’t given up substantial reality to rightly make an end of suffering.
translation_text.an6.15:3.1: As a mendicant makes their bed, so they must lie in it, and die free of regrets.
translation_text.an6.15:3.2: And how do they die free of regrets?
translation_text.an6.15:4.1: Take a mendicant who doesn’t relish work, talk, sleep, company, closeness, and proliferation. They don’t love these things or like to relish them.
translation_text.an6.15:4.6: A mendicant who makes their bed like this must lie in it, and die free of regrets.
translation_text.an6.15:4.8: a mendicant who delights in extinguishment, who has given up substantial reality to rightly make an end of suffering.
translation_text.an6.15:5.1: A beast who likes to proliferate,
translation_text.an6.15:5.4: the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.an6.15:6.1: But one who gives up proliferation,
translation_text.an6.15:6.4: the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.”
